q2_predictors.data_dimensionality: Higher-dimensional embeddings (LLM hidden states d=1536-2560, EEG d=10) produce stronger topological discrimination than low-dimensional time series (finance d=5, cardiac d=10 raw ECG)
q2_predictors.stationarity: Non-stationary systems (sleep stages, regime shifts) yield strong results. Quasi-stationary signals (financial returns) yield weak/negative results due to linear spectral dominance
q2_predictors.known_coupling: Ground-truth coupled systems (Kuramoto, tipping models) show expected binding patterns. Real-world coupling (ENSO-NAO, sleep cross-region) consistently fails to reach significance — sample size or coupling strength insufficient
q2_predictors.sample_size: Domains with 200+ samples per condition (LLM, sleep, music) achieve significance. Small samples (cardiac 15/15, hallucination 38 correct) reduce power

llm.details: Universal across Qwen, Phi-2, Pythia, StableLM

code.details: Same dip-then-rise pattern as LLM but at medium difficulty

hallucination.details: Extreme non-monotonicity; near-miss collapses to zero H1

sleep.pattern: W(4.99) < N3(5.08) < N2(5.20) < N1(5.21) < REM(5.35) — non-monotonic with depth
sleep.details: Not monotonic with sleep depth: N3 (deepest) is NOT highest H1. REM is highest.

music.details: Raw audio shows different ordering (jazz highest). Embedding transforms the ranking.

cardiac.pattern: normal(4.25) > arrhythmia(4.20) — too few conditions to test non-monotonicity

tipping.pattern: pre(3.67) ≈ post(3.72) — not enough conditions for non-monotonicity test

q3_h1_nonmonotonic.conclusion: H1 non-monotonicity is confirmed in 5/7 testable domains (LLM, code, hallucination, sleep, music). This appears to be a universal property: topological complexity does NOT scale monotonically with task difficulty or system complexity. The intermediate-difficulty minimum in LLM/code suggests a 'sweet spot' where representations are most structured.

multiagent.finding: Binding significant at K=0 (disordered); direct > indirect

q4_binding.predictors: Binding detection succeeds when: (1) ground-truth coupling exists AND is strong (Kuramoto K=0 uncoupled vs coupled), (2) population-level similarity exists (LLM representations at same difficulty). It fails when: coupling is weak (ENSO-NAO), physiological (sleep cross-region), or the joint-vs-marginal test lacks power with n=50 surrogates.

sleep.finding: Topology leads by 5s but low precision/recall (31%/16%)

cardiac.finding: Topology leads by 9.5s; decent recall (67%) but low precision (20%)

tipping_comparison.finding: Topology competitive: best for saddle-node (2291 vs 6883 var), mixed for others

climate.events: 1977 Pacific shift (yes), 1998 El Niño (no), 2016 El Niño (yes)
climate.finding: CUSUM changepoints at 1992.5, 2012.5 — not aligned with known events

finance.finding: Complete failure — topology dominated by linear spectral properties

q5_transitions.conclusion: Topology consistently LEADS transitions when the underlying dynamics are genuinely nonlinear (tipping points, sleep stage changes, arrhythmia onset). Lead times range from 5-9 seconds (physiological) to hundreds-thousands of timesteps (dynamical systems). It fails completely in linear-dominated domains (finance) and when boundaries are not dynamical transitions (music genre boundaries).
